title: Coincidental deploy near a cache-loss incident
summary: A redis-cache node is lost at 15:01, collapsing the cache hit ratio and pushing search-api onto catalog-pg, which slows search and produces timeouts. One minute after the errors begin, an unrelated search-ui frontend deploy lands at 15:03. The deploy is the obvious suspect by change-proximity, but the incident started BEFORE it and on a different component, so the deploy must be ranked low with explicit counter-evidence.
question: Why did search start failing around 15:03 on 2026-06-08, right after the search-ui deploy?

ground_truth.notes: The trap is change-proximity: a deploy at 15:03 looks causal but the symptoms began at 15:02 (and the cache loss at 15:01). The deployed service (search-ui) is not on the error path; its own error rate stays flat. Correct ranking puts redis-cache first and the deploy near zero, with counter-evidence that onset precedes the deploy.
